title: Cyclosorus nemoralis
intro: Cyclosorus nemoralis, also known as the forest lungwort, is a type of fern native to tropical Asia, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and ability to thrive in shaded environments. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus nemoralis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus nemoralis is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns with a global distribution. The classification of Cyclosorus nemoralis is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns that are characterized by their often small to medium-sized fronds. This species is part of the larger group of vascular plants.
benefits: Cyclosorus nemoralis, a type of fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. properties: Cyclosorus nemoralis is known to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.
